title: paspalum pulchellum
intro: Paspalum pulchellum, also known as dwarf paspalum, is a type of perennial grass native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Paspalum pulchellum are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
